title: University of Oslo Postdoctoral Research Fellowship 2026 in Children, Youth and Digital Media: Fully Funded Four-Year Research Opportunity in Norway
summary: The University of Oslo (UiO) is inviting applications for its prestigious Postdoctoral Research Fellowship in Children, Youth and Digital Media under the internationally recognized EU Kids Online Norway project. This outstanding research opportunity is designed for highly motivated early-career researchers who are passionate about studying the impact of digital technologies on children and young people. Hosted by the Department of Media and Communication , this four-year postdoctoral fellowship offers scholars an opportunity to conduct independent, high-impact research while collaborating with one of Europe s leading interdisciplinary research networks focused on children s digital lives. Researchers will join the Centre for Studies of Children, Youth, and Media and contribute to internationally significant studies examining how digital media shapes children s experiences, opportunities, rights, wellbeing, and online safety. If you are looking to advance your academic career while contributing to globally relevant research on digital media and youth, this fellowship presents an exceptional opportunity. About the University of Oslo The University of Oslo (UiO) is Norway s oldest and highest-ranked university. With approximately 26,500 students and over 7,200 employees , UiO is internationally recognized for excellence in education, research, and innovation. Its Department of Media and Communication is one of Scandinavia s leading academic environments dedicated to research on media, communication, politics, journalism, digital technologies, and society. Through interdisciplinary collaboration, the department contributes to global debates on communication and digital transformation. The successful fellow will become part of this vibrant academic community while collaborating closely with researchers across Europe through the EU Kids Online network. About the Fellowship The Postdoctoral Research Fellowship is attached to the EU Kids Online Norway project. EU Kids Online is one of Europe s most influential research networks studying children s digital experiences. It brings together researchers from numerous countries to investigate: Children s internet use Online opportunities Digital wellbeing Online risks Digital rights Digital literacy Media regulation Digital skills Technology and youth development The selected fellow will develop an independent research project that aligns with the objectives of EU Kids Online Norway while contributing new scientific knowledge to this rapidly evolving field. Research Areas Applicants are encouraged to propose innovative research within the broad field of children s and young people s digital lives. Relevant topics include, but are not limited to: Children s digital media use Social media and young people Digital wellbeing Online risks Cyber safety Children s online rights Media regulation Digital literacy Digital skills Internet governance Online opportunities Digital inequalities Technology and education Youth media participation Digital citizenship Projects may focus on: Individual-level factors Family influences Community-level influences National policies International comparisons Cross-country research Comparative and interdisciplinary studies are particularly welcomed. Access to Valuable Research Data One of the major advantages of this fellowship is access to extensive research resources. Depending on ethical approval and data access procedures, the fellow may gain access to: National EU Kids Online datasets International comparative datasets Existing research trajectories Established European research collaborations Using existing datasets is considered an advantage but is not mandatory . Researchers are free to propose original methodological approaches suitable for their research questions. Fellowship Responsibilities The selected researcher will undertake a range of academic responsibilities, including: Conduct Independent Research Develop and implement a high-quality research project that contributes original knowledge to the field. Participate in Academic Activities Contribute actively to: Research seminars Workshops Academic discussions Collaborative research Centre activities Research Dissemination Expected dissemination activities include: Publishing scientific articles Presenting research findings Participating in conferences Supporting public engagement activities Strengthen Research Networks The fellow will contribute to expanding both national and international collaboration within the EU Kids Online network. Teaching and Career Development Unlike many research fellowships, this position includes structured career development. The appointment lasts four years , including: 75% research 25% teaching, administration, and career development The teaching component also allows the fellow to obtain formal teaching qualifications required for future academic positions. A personalized career plan will also be developed to support long-term professional growth. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must possess: A PhD or equivalent degree in: Media and Communication Sociology Psychology Education Childhood Studies Or another closely related discipline Applicants whose doctoral thesis has been submitted for evaluation before the application deadline are also eligible, provided the dissertation is successfully defended before appointment. Candidates must also demonstrate: Excellent written English Excellent spoken English Strong motivation Personal suitability Preferred Qualifications Although not mandatory, preference may be given to applicants with experience in: Research on children and youth Digital technologies Internet studies Qualitative research Quantitative research Mixed methods Digital methods International research collaboration Interdisciplinary research University teaching Research Proposal Requirements Applicants must submit a comprehensive research proposal. The proposal should clearly describe: Research questions Objectives Literature background Theoretical framework Research methodology Data collection Data analysis Timeline Progress plan The proposal should be between 3 and 5 pages , with a maximum of 14,000 characters . Evaluation Criteria Applications will be assessed based on: Scientific Excellence Originality Innovation Academic contribution Methodological Quality Appropriate research methods Feasibility Research design Relevance Alignment with: Centre for Studies of Children, Youth, and Media EU Kids Online Norway Personal Competencies Applicants should demonstrate: Collaboration skills Communication skills Critical thinking Time management Self-motivation Ability to contribute positively to the research environment Salary and Benefits Successful applicants will receive: Annual salary between NOK 595,000 and NOK 677,000 , depending on qualifications and experience Membership in the Norwegian Public Service Pension Fund Excellent insurance benefits Attractive welfare benefits Career development support Access to world-class research facilities Opportunities to collaborate internationally Professional mentoring A stimulating interdisciplinary research environment Required Application Documents Applicants should prepare: Motivation letter Curriculum Vitae (CV) Research proposal List of publications Copies of academic certificates and diplomas (BA, MA, PhD) All documents must be submitted in English or a Scandinavian language . Why You Should Apply This fellowship offers numerous advantages, including: Four years of funded research International collaboration opportunities Participation in one of Europe s leading research networks Access to unique datasets Teaching experience Career development support Competitive salary and benefits Strong pathway toward an Associate Professor position Work at Norway s leading university Opportunities to publish internationally Visit HERE for more information about the University of Oslo Postdoctoral Research Fellowship 2026 in Children, Youth and Digital Media Application Deadline Deadline: 15 August 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the closing date to avoid last-minute technical issues. APPLY HERE Conclusion The University of Oslo s Postdoctoral Research Fellowship in Children, Youth and Digital Media is an exceptional opportunity for emerging researchers seeking to establish themselves as leaders in digital media and childhood studies. Through the internationally renowned EU Kids Online network, successful applicants will gain access to collaborative research, valuable datasets, career development opportunities, and one of Europe s strongest academic environments. If your research interests focus on children s digital experiences, online wellbeing, media regulation, or digital literacy, this fellowship provides an ideal platform to produce impactful research while advancing your academic career in Norway. title: Pathways to Scale Program 2026: Exclusive Mastercard Foundation & WUSC Business Support for Women-Led Businesses
summary: Applications are now open for the Pathways to Scale Program 2026 . Women entrepreneurs across Africa continue to play a critical role in driving economic growth, creating jobs, and transforming rural and peri-urban communities. To help women-led enterprises overcome barriers to finance, business development, and market access, the Pathways to Scale (P2S) Program is accepting applications from eligible businesses operating in Ethiopia, Ghana, Nigeria, and Rwanda . Implemented by World University Service of Canada (WUSC) in partnership with the Mastercard Foundation , the Pathways to Scale Program is a major initiative designed to strengthen women-owned and women-led businesses through tailored business support, access to finance, expert mentorship, and strategic networking opportunities. Applications for the current intake are open until 31 July 2026 . About the Pathways to Scale (P2S) Program Pathways to Scale (P2S) is a multi-country initiative that aims to help women entrepreneurs expand their businesses while increasing employment opportunities for young women across Africa. The program focuses on enterprises that have demonstrated growth potential and are positioned to scale their operations with the right combination of financial and technical support. The initiative is implemented by World University Service of Canada (WUSC) with support from the Mastercard Foundation . According to the program, P2S seeks to strengthen women-led enterprises by improving access to financing, strengthening business systems, and creating stronger connections with investors, financial institutions, and corporate partners. The broader program is expected to support thousands of women entrepreneurs while contributing to sustainable economic development across participating countries. Benefits of the Pathways to Scale Program Successful applicants will receive a comprehensive package of business support designed to accelerate enterprise growth. Benefits include: Access to financing opportunities Tailored business support powered by SIGMA™ AI Individualized mentorship from experienced business and industry experts Advisory support to strengthen business performance Opportunities to connect with investors Connections with corporate partners Linkages to financial institutions Networking opportunities within a strong ecosystem of entrepreneurs and development partners Rather than offering only financial support, the program combines funding pathways with practical business development services that help enterprises become more competitive and sustainable. Who Can Apply? The Pathways to Scale Program 2026 is designed for established women-owned and women-led businesses that are ready to scale their operations and increase their impact. The current application round targets enterprises operating in selected sectors across four African countries. To be eligible for the Pathways to Scale Program 2026 , applicants should meet the following requirements: Business Ownership Applicants must: Own or manage a women-owned or women-led business. Eligible Countries The business must be legally operating in one of the following countries: Ethiopia Ghana Nigeria Rwanda Eligible Sectors Businesses should operate in: Agriculture Manufacturing Related sectors Business Requirements Applicants should also: Demonstrate measurable impact in rural or peri-urban communities. Generate annual revenues of more than US$650,000 . Be seeking opportunities to grow their business through access to finance, tailored business support, mentorship, and strategic partnerships. Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the official eligibility criteria and complete the online application form with accurate and up-to-date business information before submitting their application. Program Focus The Pathways to Scale Program seeks businesses capable of creating long-term economic impact. Priority is given to enterprises that: Create employment opportunities. Strengthen local value chains. Support rural and peri-urban economic development. Demonstrate business growth potential. Contribute to sustainable and inclusive economic development. The program recognizes that women entrepreneurs often face greater challenges in accessing finance and investment. Through mentorship, advisory services, and investor engagement, P2S aims to reduce these barriers and enable businesses to scale successfully. Why Apply? Participating businesses gain access to more than funding alone. Successful enterprises will benefit from: Business growth support tailored to their needs. AI-powered business advisory tools through SIGMA™ AI. Guidance from experienced mentors. Increased visibility among investors and financial institutions. Expanded professional networks. Opportunities to strengthen operational systems and long-term sustainability. For businesses seeking to expand operations, improve management systems, or secure future investment, the program offers a valuable platform for growth. How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the official online application form. During the application process, applicants will be asked to: Confirm whether they are completing the application themselves or on behalf of another participant. Read and acknowledge the Data Privacy and Consent Notice. Agree to the consent terms for data sharing. Complete the eligibility and due diligence questions. Submit the required business information through the online application portal. Applicants are encouraged to prepare accurate business information before beginning the application. APPLY HERE Important Deadline Application Deadline: 31 July 2026 Late applications may not be considered. Conclusion The Pathways to Scale (P2S) Program represents an important opportunity for established women-led businesses seeking to expand their impact across Africa. By combining access to finance with mentorship, AI-powered business support, investor connections, and strategic networking, the program provides a comprehensive growth platform for enterprises operating in Ethiopia, Ghana, Nigeria, and Rwanda. Women entrepreneurs who meet the eligibility requirements are encouraged to submit their applications before the 31 July 2026 deadline and take advantage of this opportunity to strengthen their businesses and contribute to sustainable economic development. For more information about this opportunity, click here . Discover more global job opportunities, click here . title: ICSSR-JSPS Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027: Up to ₹14 Lakh Research Funding for India-Japan Collaborative Social Science Projects
summary: The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR), India , in partnership with the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (JSPS), Japan , has officially announced the Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027 . This prestigious bilateral research funding initiative aims to strengthen academic cooperation between India and Japan by supporting high-quality collaborative research projects and joint seminars in the fields of Social Sciences and Humanities . The program is designed to build sustainable international research networks, promote knowledge exchange between leading scholars, encourage the participation of early-career researchers, and address pressing social and economic challenges through collaborative research. Successful Indian research teams can receive funding of up to ₹14,00,000 over two years for Joint Research Projects or up to ₹7,00,000 for Joint Seminars. The application deadline for Indian applicants is 3 September 2026 . About the ICSSR-JSPS Joint Research Program The ICSSR-JSPS Joint Research Program is a bilateral academic cooperation initiative established under an agreement between the Indian Council of Social Science Research and the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science. The initiative seeks to foster long-term collaboration between researchers from India and Japan through: Joint Research Projects Joint Academic Seminars Research networking Exchange of expertise Development of young researchers International academic partnerships Rather than supporting isolated research activities, the program focuses on building sustainable collaborations capable of generating long-term academic and societal impact. Objectives of the Program The program has several important objectives, including: Promoting collaborative research between Indian and Japanese scholars. Strengthening academic cooperation between universities and research institutions. Supporting innovative social science and humanities research. Encouraging interdisciplinary approaches to solving social problems. Facilitating international exchange of knowledge and research expertise. Developing long-term international research networks. Supporting the participation and professional growth of young researchers. Producing research that contributes to policy development and socioeconomic advancement. Types of Research Activities Supported The FY2027 call supports two categories of collaborative activities. 1. Joint Research Projects These projects involve collaborative research conducted by teams from India and Japan. Key features include: Duration of two years Maximum of 3 projects expected to be funded Projects begin on 1 April 2027 Funding of up to ₹7,00,000 per fiscal year Maximum total funding of ₹14,00,000 per project The research must fall within: Social Sciences Humanities 2. Joint Seminars The program also funds collaborative seminars involving researchers from India and Japan. Features include: Duration of up to one week Maximum of 2 seminars Seminars must take place between 1 April 2027 and 31 March 2028 Funding up to ₹7,00,000 per seminar Seminars may be hosted in either: India Japan Research Fields The call is specifically intended for research in: Social Sciences Humanities Researchers working across interdisciplinary themes within these fields are encouraged to apply. Who Can Apply? Indian applicants must satisfy several eligibility requirements. Applicants must: Hold a permanent research or academic position. Be employed at a recognized institution. Hold a PhD or equivalent qualification. Demonstrate strong research capability. Have an established publication record or academic achievements. Be interested in conducting high-quality collaborative international research. Eligible Indian institutions include: Publicly funded universities Publicly funded colleges UGC-recognized deemed universities Public research institutes ICSSR research institutes ICSSR-recognized research institutions Visit HERE to learn more about the ICSSR-JSPS Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027. Eligibility for Japanese Researchers Japanese participants must belong to: Universities Research institutes operating under the jurisdiction of the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (JSPS). Participation Rules For Joint Research Projects: No limit on the number of participants. Researchers from third countries are not permitted . For Joint Seminars: No restriction on participant numbers. Researchers from third countries may participate only if they cover their own expenses. Funding Available Joint Research Projects ICSSR provides financial support for Indian research teams. Supported expenses include: International Travel Airfare between India and Japan Maintenance allowances are provided by JSPS. Domestic Travel Support includes: Travel expenses for Indian researchers Domestic travel costs for Japanese researchers while in India Maintenance allowances for Japanese researchers in India Other Research Costs Funding may cover: Consumables Printing Communication Honoraria Meetings Administrative research expenses ICSSR recommends that more than 50% of the project budget be allocated toward international and domestic travel. Joint Seminars in India Funding includes: Domestic travel Meeting expenses Communication Printing Reception Excursions Honoraria Seminar logistics Japanese researchers domestic expenses and maintenance allowances are covered during their stay in India. Joint Seminars in Japan ICSSR funds: International airfare Domestic travel within India for preparation meetings Communication expenses Printing Preparatory meetings Seminar organization costs in Japan are covered by the Japanese counterpart. Application Process Indian researchers must submit their applications to ICSSR. Japanese researchers must separately submit matching applications to JSPS. Applications submitted by only one side will not be considered . Applicants should therefore coordinate carefully with their international research partners before submission. APPLY HERE Application Requirements Applicants should complete the prescribed application forms provided by ICSSR. Required documents include: Completed application form Research proposal Institutional affiliation details Supporting documents required under ICSSR guidelines Researchers previously funded under ICSSR international programs during the past five years must also explain: Results achieved Expected outcomes Relevance to the new proposal Selection Criteria Applications undergo rigorous evaluation. Assessment criteria include: Scientific excellence. Quality of research proposal. Importance of international collaboration. Knowledge transfer potential. Contribution to solving social challenges. Socioeconomic impact. Participation of young researchers. Feasibility of implementation. Research methodology. Policy relevance. Budget appropriateness. Suitability of seminar venue (where applicable). Review committees from ICSSR and JSPS may conduct separate evaluations before jointly selecting successful proposals. Obligations of Selected Researchers Successful Indian team leaders must: Submit implementation plans. Request project funding using prescribed forms. Administer funds through their affiliated institutions. Submit final reports. Publicly acknowledge ICSSR and JSPS support in publications. Complete research ethics education (for research projects). Ethical Responsibilities ICSSR maintains strict standards regarding research integrity. Funding may be terminated if researchers engage in: Fabrication Falsification Plagiarism Misuse of funds Harassment Discrimination Violations of applicable laws Researchers are expected to maintain the highest ethical standards throughout the project. Intellectual Property Project leaders from both countries must ensure: Proper protection of intellectual property Fair distribution of research outputs Compliance with national regulations Institutions are encouraged to establish appropriate intellectual property policies before the project begins. Important Notes Applicants should keep the following in mind: Matching applications are mandatory from both India and Japan. Projects start from 1 April 2027 . Selection numbers may change depending on available budgets. ICSSR does not arrange visas or accommodation. ICSSR is not responsible for accidents or medical emergencies during project-related travel. Reports submitted may be published on the ICSSR website. Application Deadline Deadline for Indian researchers: 3 September 2026 Late applications are not expected to be accepted. Applicants are encouraged to coordinate with their Japanese counterparts well in advance to ensure both submissions are completed before the deadline. Conclusion The ICSSR-JSPS Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027 represents an outstanding opportunity for experienced researchers in India and Japan to establish meaningful international collaborations in the social sciences and humanities. Beyond financial support, the initiative promotes long-term academic partnerships, interdisciplinary research, knowledge exchange, and the development of early-career researchers. Institutions and scholars seeking to address important societal challenges through collaborative research should take advantage of this prestigious bilateral funding opportunity by preparing strong proposals and ensuring coordinated submissions from both partner countries before the application deadline of 3 September 2026 . title: Coordinator for ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme (2026): Remote Opportunity to Lead Europe’s Next Generation of Cancer Prevention Advocates
summary: The Association of European Cancer Leagues (ECL) is inviting applications for the position of Coordinator for the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme . This exciting part-time opportunity is designed for passionate professionals who are interested in cancer prevention, youth engagement, leadership, and international collaboration. The successful candidate will play a central role in coordinating one of Europe s leading youth advocacy initiatives focused on cancer prevention. Working closely with the ECL Secretariat and Board, the Coordinator will oversee the implementation of the programme s strategic goals while supporting young advocates from across Europe. This role offers flexible remote working arrangements, competitive hourly compensation, and the opportunity to contribute to a meaningful public health mission that has positively impacted young people from more than 30 European countries. About the Association of European Cancer Leagues (ECL) The Association of European Cancer Leagues (ECL) is a respected non-profit umbrella organization representing 35 national and regional cancer leagues across Europe . The organization works to improve cancer prevention, treatment, patient care, and public health policies throughout Europe. Its vision is straightforward but ambitious: A Europe free of cancer. ECL members include trusted non-profit organizations that provide: Cancer prevention education Patient support services Public awareness campaigns Advocacy for stronger cancer policies Information and healthcare resources ECL also maintains strict ethical standards by refusing financial support from the tobacco industry and other companies whose products are considered harmful to health. About the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme Launched in 2015 , the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme has become one of Europe s leading youth-led cancer prevention initiatives. The programme brings together enthusiastic young people committed to improving public health through awareness, education, advocacy, and policy engagement. Its primary educational framework is the European Code Against Cancer , which serves as a practical guide for reducing cancer risks through healthy lifestyle choices and preventive measures. Since its establishment, the programme has successfully built a network of youth advocates from more than 30 European countries . Participants come from diverse academic and professional backgrounds including: Medicine Public Health Political Science Law Health Policy Social Sciences Research Other health-related disciplines Throughout the year, Youth Ambassadors participate in: Public awareness campaigns International events Educational workshops Online advocacy initiatives Summer Schools Networking activities Policy discussions Community engagement projects The programme empowers young leaders to promote cancer prevention at both national and European Union levels. About the Coordinator Position ECL is seeking a part-time Youth Ambassadors Programme Coordinator who will provide operational leadership and strategic coordination for the programme. The selected individual will work closely with: The ECL Secretariat The ECL Board The Senior Policy Officer for Cancer Prevention Youth Ambassadors across Europe The Coordinator will ensure that the programme continues to grow while successfully implementing its strategic objectives and annual action plans. Although the initial contract lasts for one year , ECL intends to establish a longer-term working relationship with the successful candidate, subject to performance and future collaboration opportunities. Key Responsibilities The Coordinator will perform a wide range of leadership, planning, and administrative responsibilities. These include: 1. Programme Coordination The Coordinator will oversee the day-to-day management of the Youth Ambassadors Programme. Responsibilities include: Coordinating programme activities Supporting Youth Ambassadors Maintaining effective communication with members Facilitating collaboration among participants 2. Strategic Leadership The successful candidate will help implement the programme s long-term strategy by: Drafting Action Plans Implementing strategic objectives Monitoring programme progress Reporting on programme achievements Evaluating outcomes and future priorities 3. Funding Support The Coordinator will work with the Secretariat to identify and support funding opportunities that strengthen the programme s sustainability. Tasks may include: Researching funding opportunities Assisting with grant-related activities Supporting project development 4. Event Organisation The Coordinator will organize major programme activities including: Annual Summer School Youth Ambassador meetings Workshops Online events Additional programme activities when required. Who Should Apply? ECL is searching for an organized, motivated, and reliable individual with a passion for youth leadership and cancer prevention. Applicants should demonstrate the ability to work independently while collaborating with an international team. Priority consideration will be given to current or former members of the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme , although applications from all qualified candidates are encouraged. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should possess the following qualifications: A university degree Familiarity with the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme Strong organizational skills Excellent written and spoken English High levels of trustworthiness and reliability Experience in or strong interest in cancer prevention Ability to work in an international and multicultural environment Benefits of the Position Successful applicants will receive several professional benefits, including: One-year part-time contract Possibility of contract extension Flexible remote working arrangements Opportunity to work from Brussels, Belgium Competitive hourly rate of €30 Flexible workload of approximately 10–20 hours per month Opportunity to collaborate with leading European public health professionals Valuable international networking experience Leadership experience within a major European non-profit organization Contribution to cancer prevention initiatives across Europe Work Location Applicants may choose between: Remote work Brussels, Belgium The flexible work arrangement allows candidates from different locations to contribute effectively while maintaining work-life balance. Contract Details Contract Duration: One year Extension: Possible based on future collaboration Employment Type: Part-time freelance consultancy Expected Start Date: As soon as possible Workload: Approximately 10–20 hours per month Hourly Rate: €30 How to Apply Interested applicants should prepare the following documents: Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV) Two professional references Letter of motivation Applications should be submitted via email: vacancy@cancer.eu Email Subject Line: Application – YA coordinator The deadline for submitting applications is 22 July 2026 . Learn more about this job HERE Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible because: Interviews will be conducted on a rolling basis . Shortlisted candidates will be contacted within one week after the application deadline. Due to the anticipated high volume of applications, ECL will not provide individual feedback to unsuccessful applicants. Why You Should Consider This Opportunity This position offers much more than a part-time consultancy role. It provides an opportunity to contribute directly to one of Europe s most impactful youth health advocacy programmes. Professionals interested in public health, cancer prevention, youth empowerment, international cooperation, and policy advocacy will gain valuable experience working with experts and young leaders from across Europe. The role also offers flexibility, international exposure, leadership experience, and the chance to make a meaningful difference in cancer prevention efforts that benefit communities across Europe. Whether you have previously participated in the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme or are an experienced professional with a passion for public health, this position provides an excellent platform for advancing your career while contributing to an important cause. Conclusion The Coordinator for the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme is an outstanding opportunity for individuals who are passionate about youth leadership, public health, and cancer prevention. With flexible remote working options, competitive compensation, and the opportunity to coordinate an influential international programme, this role is ideal for professionals seeking meaningful impact within the European public health sector. Interested candidates should ensure they submit a complete application before the 22 July 2026 deadline to maximize their chances of consideration.
